_meta.coverage_note: FULL IMPORT — all 30 November dates. 12 dates carried from Menaion v1 pilot (2026-03-07); 18 dates added in tranche 2 (2026-03-08). Source posture: OCA/Antiochian Hapgood-tradition English and Jordanville Menaion. Dates with no ranked commemoration in the tradition carry troparion_status:'text-unavailable' where a commemorand is known but text is not confirmed from a published source, or are absent where the date falls between feasts without a ranked entry in the new-calendar corpus.

commemorations.name: Holy Unmercenaries Cosmas and Damian of Asia

troparion.title: Troparion of Cosmas and Damian of Asia — Tone 8
troparion.text: Holy unmercenaries and wonderworkers, Cosmas and Damian, visit our infirmities. Freely ye have received, freely give to us.

commemorations.notes: Asian Cosmas and Damian; distinguished from the Roman (July 1) and Arabian (November 1 OS / new calendar varies) pairs. Rank 3 / Doxology.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Acindynus, Pegasius, Anempodistus, Elpidiphorus, and Aphthonius
commemorations.notes: Persian martyrs under Shapur II. Rank 4 / Simple. Troparion text not confirmed from published Antiochian/OCA source for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Acepsimas, Bishop of Persia, and his Companions
commemorations.notes: Persian episcopate martyrs. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Venerable Joannicius the Great

troparion.title: Troparion of Joannicius the Great — Tone 8
troparion.text: By a flood of tears thou didst make the desert fertile, and thy longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance by thy sighs from the depths of thy soul; thou didst become a light shining over the whole world. O our holy Father Joannicius, pray to Christ God to save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Great ascetic of Bithynia, 754–846. Rank 3 / Doxology.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Galacteon and Episteme
commemorations.notes: Husband and wife martyrs. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Paul the Confessor, Patriarch of Constantinople

troparion.title: Troparion of Paul the Confessor — Tone 8
troparion.text: O champion of Orthodoxy and teacher of piety, thou didst shine forth as a pillar and foundation of the Church, adorning the hierarchical throne of Constantinople. O holy Father Paul the Confessor, entreat Christ God to grant us His great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Patriarch of Constantinople, exiled and martyred for opposing Arianism, +350. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: The Thirty-Three Martyrs of Melitene
commemorations.notes: Martyrs under Diocletian at Melitene in Armenia. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Synaxis of the Chief of the Heavenly Hosts, Archangel Michael and the Other Bodiless Powers

troparion.title: Troparion of the Archangels — Tone 4
troparion.text: O Commanders of the Heavenly Hosts, we who are unworthy beseech you, that through your entreaties you will encircle us with the protection of the wings of your immaterial glory, and guard us who fall down and cry to you: Deliver us from all harm, as commanders of the powers on high.

commemorations.notes: Chief feast of the Bodiless Hosts. Rank 2 / Polyeleos. Primary test date for menaion-resolved path.

commemorations.name: Miracle of Archangel Michael at Chonae (Colossae)

troparion.title: Troparion of the Miracle at Chonae — Tone 4
troparion.text: O Commanders of the Heavenly Hosts, we who are unworthy beseech you, that through your entreaties you will encircle us with the protection of the wings of your immaterial glory, and guard us who fall down and cry to you: Deliver us from all harm, as commanders of the powers on high.

commemorations.notes: Dedication of church at Chonae; miracle of the spring. Rank 4. Troparion is the Archangel troparion (same as Nov 8, tone 4), as is standard practice.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Orestes and Erasmus of Ohrid
commemorations.notes: November 10 in the new-calendar Menaion also commemorates the Holy Martyr Orestes. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs Menas, Victor, and Vincent
commemorations.notes: Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: John the Merciful, Patriarch of Alexandria

troparion.title: Troparion of John the Merciful — Tone 8
troparion.text: In thee, O Father John, was exactly preserved that which was according to the image of God; for taking up thy cross thou didst follow Christ, teaching by deeds to despise the flesh since it passes away, and to care for the soul as an immortal thing. Therefore thy soul rejoiceth with the angels.

commemorations.notes: Patriarch of Alexandria, great almsgiver and wonderworker, +619. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Our Father among the Saints John Chrysostom, Archbishop of Constantinople

troparion.title: Troparion of John Chrysostom — Tone 8
troparion.text: Grace shining forth from thy lips like a beacon hath enlightened the universe; it hath shown to the world the riches of poverty; it hath revealed the heights of humility; teaching us by thy words, O Father John Chrysostom, intercede before the Word, Christ our God, to save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Repose of St. John Chrysostom, +407. Rank 2 / Polyeleos. Second commemoration: Nov 13 also has St. Arcadius of Cyprus (rank 4) — John Chrysostom takes the troparion slot.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Philip

troparion.title: Troparion of the Apostle Philip — Tone 8
troparion.text: Thou wast a disciple of the light, and like an eagle soaring high, thou didst proclaim the Logos, the Pre-eternal Word, Who took flesh of the Virgin. O Apostle Philip, intercede with Christ our God to save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Feast of Holy Apostle Philip. Rank 2 / Polyeleos. Philip Fast begins the following day (Nov 15).

commemorations.name: Holy Martyrs and Confessors of Persia
commemorations.notes: November 15 commemorates a group of Persian martyrs. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus. Note: November 15 is also the start of the Philip Fast (Advent fast) in Orthodox practice.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle and Evangelist Matthew

troparion.title: Troparion of the Apostle Matthew — Tone 3
troparion.text: With eagerness leaving the tax collector's table, thou didst follow Christ the Master and, receiving the grace of the apostolate, thou didst preach the Gospel to all; and wondrously writing it down, thou didst draw souls unto salvation. By thy prayer, O Apostle Matthew, grant us great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Feast of Holy Apostle and Evangelist Matthew. Rank 2 / Polyeleos.

commemorations.name: Our Father among the Saints Gregory the Wonderworker, Bishop of Neo-Caesarea

troparion.title: Troparion of Gregory the Wonderworker — Tone 8
troparion.text: As a rule of faith and image of meekness, the truth of things hath shown thee to thy flock; wherefore thou hast gained the heights of humility through poverty, the riches through thy mighty deeds; O Father Gregory, pray to Christ God that our souls may be saved.

commemorations.notes: Gregory Thaumaturgos, bishop of Neocaesarea, disciple of Origen, +270. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Plato of Ancyra
commemorations.notes: Martyr at Ancyra under Diocletian. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Holy Prophet Obadiah (Abdias)
commemorations.notes: Prophet Obadiah. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Forefeast of the Entrance of the Most Holy Theotokos into the Temple

troparion.title: Troparion of the Forefeast of the Entrance — Tone 4
troparion.text: Today is the prelude of God's good will and the proclamation of the salvation of men. The Virgin appears in the temple of God, and foretells Christ to all. Let us also cry out to her with a mighty voice: Rejoice, thou fulfilment of the Creator's dispensation.

commemorations.notes: Forefeast of the Entrance of the Theotokos. Rank 4. Troparion is the standard forefeast troparion.

commemorations.name: The Entrance of the Most Holy Theotokos into the Temple

troparion.title: Troparion of the Entrance of the Theotokos — Tone 4
troparion.text: Today is the prelude of God's good will and the proclamation of the salvation of men. The Virgin appears in the temple of God, and foretells Christ to all. Let us also cry out to her with a mighty voice: Rejoice, thou fulfilment of the Creator's dispensation.

commemorations.notes: One of the Twelve Great Feasts. Rank 1. Great Feast — highest priority; Octoechos displaced entirely.

commemorations.name: Afterfeast of the Entrance of the Most Holy Theotokos into the Temple

troparion.title: Troparion of the Afterfeast of the Entrance — Tone 4
troparion.text: Today is the prelude of God's good will and the proclamation of the salvation of men. The Virgin appears in the temple of God, and foretells Christ to all. Let us also cry out to her with a mighty voice: Rejoice, thou fulfilment of the Creator's dispensation.

commemorations.notes: First day of the Afterfeast of the Entrance. Rank 4. Troparion is the feast troparion repeated during the afterfeast (standard practice).

commemorations.name: Our Father among the Saints Amphilochius, Bishop of Iconium
commemorations.notes: Amphilochius of Iconium, friend of the Cappadocian Fathers, +394. Rank 3. Also: Afterfeast of Entrance continues. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus; the feast troparion (Entrance) would normally be sung alongside.

commemorations.name: Holy Great Martyr Catherine of Alexandria

troparion.title: Troparion of the Great Martyr Catherine — Tone 4
troparion.text: Let us praise the wise Catherine, the praised and honorable bride of Christ, who contended for Him in a contest of faith, silencing the wisdom of the ungodly by the power of the Holy Spirit; and, being crowned with martyrdom, she is entreating mercy for all.

commemorations.notes: Holy Great Martyr and All-Wise Catherine of Alexandria. Rank 3 / Doxology. Also: Afterfeast of Entrance continues.

commemorations.name: Our Father among the Saints Clement, Pope of Rome

troparion.title: Troparion of Clement of Rome — Tone 4
troparion.text: As a successor of the apostles and throne-sharer, thou wast a teacher of the world, O Clement; in all things thou didst establish what is needful for the soul. Thou didst struggle for the Truth unto blood, O Holy Hieromartyr Clement; entreat Christ God to save our souls.

commemorations.notes: Hieromartyr Clement, Pope of Rome, +101. Rank 3. Nov 25 is also the Leavetaking (Apodosis) of the Entrance of the Theotokos.

commemorations.name: Leavetaking of the Entrance of the Most Holy Theotokos

troparion.title: Troparion of the Leavetaking of the Entrance — Tone 4
troparion.text: Today is the prelude of God's good will and the proclamation of the salvation of men. The Virgin appears in the temple of God, and foretells Christ to all. Let us also cry out to her with a mighty voice: Rejoice, thou fulfilment of the Creator's dispensation.

commemorations.notes: Leavetaking (Apodosis) of the Entrance of the Theotokos. Rank 3. Two rank-3 entries: resolver will select Clement (first entry, traditional precedence). Engine feast-rank override system (deferred) will handle proper apodosis weighting.

commemorations.name: Venerable Alypius the Stylite
commemorations.notes: Alypius the Stylite of Adrianopolis, +640. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Holy Great Martyr James the Persian

troparion.title: Troparion of James the Persian — Tone 4
troparion.text: Thy martyr James, O Lord, received the crown of incorruption from Thee, our God, for his struggle; for, possessed of Thy strength, he set aside the tyrants and crushed the feeble opposition of demons. Through his prayers, save Thou our souls.

commemorations.notes: Holy Great Martyr James Intercisus (the Persian), +421. Rank 3.

commemorations.name: Our Father among the Saints Stephen the Younger, Martyr
commemorations.notes: Stephen the Younger, martyr under Constantine V Copronymus for veneration of icons, +764. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Holy Martyr Paramon and his Three Hundred and Seventy Companions
commemorations.notes: Martyrs in Bithynia under Decius. Rank 4. Troparion text not confirmed for this corpus.

commemorations.name: Holy Apostle Andrew the First-Called

troparion.title: Troparion of the Apostle Andrew — Tone 4
troparion.text: As the first-called of the apostles and the brother of the foremost disciple, entreat the Master of all, O Andrew, to grant peace to the world and to our souls great mercy.

commemorations.notes: Holy Apostle Andrew the First-Called. Rank 2 / Polyeleos. Patron of Constantinople, Greece, Russia, and Scotland.
